summary: Large unstubbing/matching pass across remaining unmatched code in fighter, game-mode, stage, sound, bonus, and Dolphin THP decode code. The PR added 3659 lines and removed 193 across 28 files, replacing many `/// #` stubs with C implementations and related header/type fixes. decomp.dev reported +3428 matched code bytes, +48 matched data bytes, 10 new 100% matches, and 39 improved unmatched items, with 4 small unmatched regressions.
what_changed: Added new THP decoder implementations/prototypes including `THPDec_8032F8D4`, `THPDec_80330158`, `THPDec_803310CC`, `THPDec_80331340`, and `THPDec_803313D0`; adjusted `THPFileInfo` by replacing part of padding with `u8* x0C`.
Replaced several ftCommon catch/attack100-related stubs with C, including `ftCo_800D730C`, `fn_800D9558`, `fn_800D8EC8`, `fn_800D9930`, `fn_800DA054`, `fn_800DA8E4`, and `ftCo_CapturePulledHi_Phys`.

Filled Kirby special-N and special-Lw collision/IASA/animation logic including `ftKb_SpecialLw1_Coll`, `ftKb_SpecialAirLwStart_Coll`, `ftKb_SpecialAirLw_Coll`, `ftKb_SpecialAirLwEnd_Coll`, `ftKb_SpecialNSpit0_Anim`, `ftKb_SpecialNSpit1_Anim`, `ftKb_EatWait_IASA`, `ftKb_SpecialAirNCaptureWait_IASA`, and `fn_800F9260`.

Added or improved game-mode functions in `gm_1601.c`, `gm_18A5.c`, `gm_1B03.c`, and `gm_1BA8.c`, including `fn_80161154`, `gm_80166378`, `fn_8018B090`, `gm_801B0DD0`, `gm_801B1C24`, `gm_801BAD70`, and `gm_801BDE94`.
Added stage, soundtest, audio, bonus, dynamics, and collision decomp work in `grinishie1`, `grflatzone`, `soundtest`, `lbaudio_ax`, `plbonuslib`, `ftdynamics`, and `ftcoll`.
Updated several type/header files for matching support, including fighter character type headers, `pl/types.h`, `gm_1601.h`, `soundtest.h`, `lbmthp.h`, `plbonuslib.h`, and synth headers.
smart_moves: Kept THP unknown layouts local and offset-driven by introducing `THPScanComp`, `THPScanInfo`, and `THPDecodeInfo` with explicit padding and named offset fields, allowing useful C progress without overclaiming semantic names.
Exposed only the THP functions needed by other code in `thp.h` while preserving unknown names such as `THPDec_8032F8D4`, avoiding speculative renames.
Used `#ifdef __MWERKS__` inline assembly in THP decode setup to write GQR5/GQR6 while keeping the source buildable under non-Metrowerks tooling.
Preserved matching-sensitive details such as explicit casts, float comparisons against attribute fields, `PAD_STACK`, `UNUSED` locals, callback casts, and manual pointer increments in parser code.
Added minimal struct/header fields where needed, e.g. `THPFileInfo.x0C` and `ftCaptain` motion-var field `speciallw.x4`, instead of broad refactors.
decomp_lessons: For SDK-like parser/decode code, matching often depends on modeling stream pointer movement exactly rather than simplifying into cleaner loops or library calls.
When struct semantics are still unknown, offset-style names like `x8CC`, `x8D4`, `pad8D6`, and `components[3]` are acceptable if they document layout and unblock matching.
Partial matches across many files can still be valuable: the bot recorded 39 improvements even though only 10 items became new 100% matches.
Header changes should be tied to demonstrated use in the diff; this PR added THP prototypes and layout fields alongside implementations that consume them.
Item/fighter interaction decomp often needs cross-system includes (`itlinkhookshot`, `itsamusgrapple`, `mplib`, effects, JObj matrix helpers) and careful callback typing.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions retained address-based project names such as `fn_800D9558`, `gm_801BAD70`, `un_802FFF2C`, and `THPDec_803310CC`.

Known behavior names were used where already established, such as `ftKb_SpecialLw1_Coll`, `ftKb_SpecialNSpit0_Anim`, `ftCo_CapturePulledHi_Phys`, `HSD_SynthSFXSampleLoadCallback`, and `grInishie1_801FC664`.
Unknown struct members used offset-derived names and padding arrays (`x0C`, `x70`, `x8F0`, `pad7D`, `pad8EC`) rather than speculative semantic labels.
Local scratch variables in matching code used register-style or generic names (`r29`, `r4`, `grav`, `var_f3`, `mtxY`) where semantics were incomplete.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used Metrowerks-only inline assembly blocks to set paired-single quantization registers GQR5 and GQR6 before THP decompression loops.
Inserted explicit padding and stack-control markers such as `PAD_STACK(0x14)`, `PAD_STACK(0x8)`, and `UNUSED` locals to preserve code generation.
Used explicit function-pointer casts for callbacks like Samus grapple callbacks where type signatures did not line up exactly.
Preserved manual byte parsing in JPEG/JFIF/THP routines, including marker checks, `len`-based pointer advancement, and early numeric return codes.
Matched floating-point control flow by comparing frame counters stored as floats against casted attribute fields, rather than converting logic to integers.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev reported matched code increased to 69.77% (+0.09%, +3428 bytes) and matched data to 41.27% (+48 bytes).

decomp.dev listed 10 new matches, including `grInishie1_801FC664`, `ftKb_SpecialLw1_Coll`, `fn_800D9CE8`, `fn_800DA8E4`, `ftKb_SpecialAirLwEnd_Coll`, `fn_800DA054`, `ftKb_SpecialNSpit0_Anim`, `ftKb_SpecialNSpit1_Anim`, `ftCo_CapturePulledHi_Phys`, and THPDec `.sdata2`.
decomp.dev also noted 4 unmatched regressions: `lbAudioAx_80028690`, `gm_1BA8` `.sdata2`, `un_80300B58`, and `lbAudioAx_8002838C`.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching `THPDec_8032F8D4` or `THPDec_803310CC` for improved names and full matches.
Search for follow-up fixes to the reported unmatched regressions in `lbaudio_ax`, `gm_1BA8` `.sdata2`, and `soundtest`.
Look for later type refinements to `THPScanInfo`, `THPDecodeInfo`, `ftSs_DatAttrs`, and `ftLk_DatAttrs`.
Find PRs that finish the partial matches listed by decomp.dev, especially `fn_8018B090`, `fn_80161154`, `gm_801BAD70`, and `HSD_Synth_8038A000`.
